paper_title: A Novel Method Based on Backscattering for Discriminating Summer Blooms of the Raphidophyte (Chattonella spp.) and the Diatom (Skeletonema spp.) Using MODIS Images in Ariake Sea, Japan

methods.description: Collection and analysis of field data during the summer season to identify bloom conditions based on cellular abundance thresholds.

steps.description: Field data during the summer season (June to September) were collected from Saga Fisheries Promotion Center and Kumamoto Fisheries Research Center (from 2011-2014).
steps.searchable_summary: collect field data from fisheries centers for summer season

steps.description: Field data from 2011 to 2014 were divided into three data groups according to the bloom conditions.
steps.searchable_summary: divide field data into bloom condition groups

steps.description: In addition to the field data, a number of bloom reports were also obtained from the Japan Red Tide Online site for the period 2015-2018 for algorithm validation.
steps.searchable_summary: obtain bloom reports for algorithm validation

methods.description: Processing and analysis of MODIS-Aqua satellite data to match with field data and derive inherent optical properties for bloom discrimination.

steps.description: MODIS-Aqua level 2 values were downloaded from https://oceancolor.gsfc.nasa.gov/ and extracted from locations where field data was collected in 2011-2014 using Windows Image Manager (WIM) software.
steps.searchable_summary: download and extract MODIS-Aqua data using WIM

steps.description: The routine wam_match within WIM was used to find matches between in situ measurements and satellite image data.
steps.searchable_summary: match in situ measurements with satellite data using wam_match

steps.description: Mean value of the valid pixels within the 3 × 3 windows were used as the final remote sensing reflectance.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate mean value of valid pixels for reflectance

steps.description: Flags were used to control the quality of the MODIS data.
steps.searchable_summary: apply quality control flags to MODIS data

steps.description: Inherent optical properties (IOPs) like total absorption and particle backscattering were derived using MODIS as input into the Quasi-Analytical Algorithm (QAA Version 6).
steps.searchable_summary: derive IOPs using QAA with MODIS data
